title: Smoky British Mulukhiyah Sunday Roast
description: A hearty, creamy Sunday roast featuring the unique flavors of mulukhiyah, a traditional British-Scottish dish.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, cook the smoked bacon over medium heat until crispy, then set aside.
Trim the pork loin and season with salt, black pepper, thyme, and rosemary.
Sear the pork loin in the same pot as the bacon until browned on all sides.
Remove the pork loin and set aside with the bacon.
Add the onions and garlic to the pot and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the all-purpose flour and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually add the braised red wine and chicken broth, whisking continuously.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then add the pork loin back to the pot, cover, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20 minutes, then reduce the heat to 350°F (175°C) and continue cooking for an additional 40-50 minutes, or until the pork loin reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
In a separate saucepan, combine the heavy cream and the juices from the pot, then simmer over low heat until reduced and thickened.
Shred the pork loin with two forks, then toss with the creamy sauce and chopped fresh herbs.
Serve the smoky mulukhiyah Sunday roast in individual portions, garnished with additional fresh herbs if desired.
Garnish with roasted vegetables, if desired.
Garnish with additional fresh herbs, if desired.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
